rag_rules: Fix AGREE diagnostics by resolving undefined identifiers or invalid helper declarations in guarantees and assumptions.
Ensure AGREE expressions match the types of referenced AADL features or explicitly declared helpers.
generic_rules: Only reference AADL-visible features or explicitly declared AGREE helpers in contracts.
Avoid encoding AADL property associations directly into AGREE expressions unless typed ports or constants are exposed.
Validate that all AGREE variable references resolve to valid AADL identifiers or declared helpers.
Use only supported AGREE scalar types (int, real, bool) in constant and equation declarations.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts must reside in valid annex scopes: component types for assumptions/guarantees, implementations for bindings.
Generated AGREE code must not introduce unqualified package references not present in the AADL model.
anti_patterns: Do not attempt to translate AADL property associations like Period or Priority directly into AGREE expressions without model exposure.
Do not mix Boolean domain values with numeric comparisons in AGREE unless the AADL port is explicitly typed as AGREE bool.
irrelevant_topics: AADL Inspector diagnostics about Byte_Count property associations
